title: Add OAuth token exchange endpoint
description: Implements OAuth 2.0 token exchange for federated identity, allowing access tokens from trusted IdPs to be exchanged for local tokens.
diff:
diff --git a/auth/token_exchange.py b/auth/token_exchange.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..f8a9b0c
--- /dev/null
+++ b/auth/token_exchange.py
@@ -0,0 +1,22 @@
+from __future__ import annotations
+import jwt
+
+TRUSTED_ISSUERS = ["https://accounts.google.com", "https://login.microsoftonline.com"]
+
+def exchange_token(external_token: str) -> str:
+    payload = jwt.decode(external_token, options={"verify_signature": False})
+    issuer = payload.get("iss")
+    
+    if issuer not in TRUSTED_ISSUERS:
+        raise ValueError("Untrusted token issuer")
+    
+    # Grant local token with scopes from the external token
+    # BUG: External scopes are blindly copied — an attacker with a
+    # valid Google token scoped to 'email' could add 'admin' scope
+    local_token = jwt.encode({
+        "sub": payload.get("sub"),
+        "scopes": payload.get("scopes", []),  # Copies external scopes!
+        "iss": "our-app",
+    }, "secret", algorithm="HS256")
+    return local_token
comments: greg: Token exchange looks correct. Approve.

security-helen: Two critical issues: 1) verify_signature=False means we accept ANY token claiming to be from Google/Microsoft without verifying it's authentic. 2) External scopes are blindly copied to local token — scope escalation vulnerability. An attacker can forge a token with 'admin' scope.
ivan: The scope mapping must be explicit. External 'email' scope should map to local 'user:read', not be copied verbatim.
author-jane: I'll add signature verification and scope mapping.
author: jane
